title: Bitter Aerated Chinese Rice
description: A unique, egg-free-friendly Chinese dish that combines bitter flavors with aerated texture and polish presentation, perfect for a date night.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add ground white pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add short-grain rice and stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add rice wine and cook until absorbed, then add mirin and stir-fry for another minute.
Add pickled ginger and cucumber, then stir-fry until the mixture is well combined.
Add soy sauce and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the liquid is almost completely absorbed.
Stir in salt and sugar, then add bitter melon and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly, then aerate the rice mixture with a fork to create a light and fluffy texture.
Transfer the rice mixture to a serving platter and serve immediately.
